questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
If $\lim _{x \rightarrow} x \sin \left(\frac{1}{x}\right)=A$ and $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} x \sin \left(\frac{1}{x}\right)=B$,
then which one of the following is correct?
questions.solution:
As given,
$\mathrm{A}=\lim _{\mathrm{x} \rightarrow \infty} \mathrm{x} \sin \left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}\right)=\lim _{\mathrm{x} \rightarrow \infty} \frac{\sin \left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}\right)}{\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}\right)}$
Let $\mathrm{t}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}$ when $\mathrm{x} \rightarrow \alpha, \mathrm{t} \rightarrow 0$
$\begin{aligned}
\Rightarrow \mathrm{A} &=\lim _{t \rightarrow \infty} \frac{\sin \mathrm{t}}{\mathrm{t}}=1 \\
&\left[\because \lim _{\mathrm{t} \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin \mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{x}}=1\right]
\end{aligned}$
and $\mathrm{B}=\lim _{\mathrm{x} \rightarrow 0} \mathrm{x} \sin \left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}\right)$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{B}=\lim _{\mathrm{x} \rightarrow 0} \mathrm{x} \cdot \lim _{\mathrm{x} \rightarrow 0} \sin \left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}\right)$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{B}=0$
$\therefore \quad A=1$ and $B=0$ is correct
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{x^{2}-2 x+1}{x^{2}-4 x+2}\right)^{x}$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \lim _{x \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{x^{2}-2 x+1}{x^{2}-4 x+2}\right)^{x} \\ &=\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty}\left(1+\frac{2 x-1}{x^{2}-4 x+2}\right)^{x} \\ &=e^{\lim _{x \rightarrow-\infty}\left(\frac{x(2 x-1)}{x^{2}-4 x+2}\right)}=e^{2} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The left-hand derivative of $\mathrm{f}(x)=[x] \sin (\pi x)$, at $x=\mathrm{k}, \mathrm{k}$ is an integer and $[\cdot]$ is the greatest integer function, is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} f(x)= & {[x] \sin (\pi x) } \\ \text { LHD } & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(k-h)-f(k)}{-h} \\ & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{[k-h] \sin \pi(k-h)-[k] \sin k \pi}{-h} \\ & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{(k-1) \sin (k \pi-\pi h)-k \sin k \pi}{-h} \\ & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{(-1)^{k+1}(k-1) \sinh \pi-0}{-h} \quad \ldots[\because k \in I] \\ & =(-1)^k(k-1) \pi\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If $l=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{x}{|x|+x^2}$, then the value of $l$ is
questions.solution:
Let $\mathrm{f}(x)=\frac{x}{|x|+x^2}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{-}} \mathrm{f}(x)=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{x}{-x+x^2}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{1}{-1+x}=-1 \\
& \lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{+}} \mathrm{f}(x)=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{x}{x+x^2}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{1}{1+x}=1
\end{aligned}$
Here, $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{-}} \mathrm{f}(x) \neq \lim _{x \rightarrow 0^{+}} \mathrm{f}(x)$
$\therefore \quad$ Value of $l$ is non-existant
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of $\operatorname{Lt}_{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{1+5 x^2}{1+3 x^2}\right)^{\frac{1}{x^2}}$ is
questions.solution: Hints: $\operatorname{Lim}_{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{1+5 x^2}{1+3 x^2}\right)^{\frac{1}{x^2}}=\mathrm{e}^{\operatorname{Lim}_{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{1}{x^2}\left(\frac{1+5 x^2}{1+3 x^2}-1\right)}=\mathrm{e}^{\operatorname{Lim}_{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{2 x^2}{x^2\left(1+3 x^2\right)}}=\mathrm{e}^2$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\mathrm{I}=\lim _{\mathrm{x} \rightarrow 0} \sin \left(\frac{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}-\mathrm{x}-1-\frac{\mathrm{x}^{2}}{2}}{\mathrm{x}^{2}}\right)$, then limit
questions.solution: $I=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \sin \left(\frac{e^{x}-x-1-\frac{x^{2}}{2}}{x^{2}}\right)=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{e^{x}-x-1-\frac{x^{2}}{2}}{x^{2}}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{e^{x}-1-0-x}{2 x}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{e^{x}-1}{2}=0$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Let $f(x)$ be a differentiable function and $f^{\prime}(4)=5 .$ Then, $\lim _{x \rightarrow 2} \frac{f(4) - f\left(x^{2}\right)}{x-2}$ equals
questions.solution:
Given, $f^{\prime}(4)=5$
$$
\begin{array}{ll}
\text { Now, } \lim _{x \rightarrow 2} \frac{f(4)-f\left(x^{2}\right)}{x-2} & {\left[\frac{0}{0} \text { form }\right]} \\
=\lim _{x \rightarrow 2} \frac{0-f^{\prime}\left(x^{2}\right) \cdot 2 x}{1} \\
=\frac{-f^{\prime}(4) \cdot 2 \times 2}{1} \\
=-(5) \times 4=-20
\end{array}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: $\lim _{x \rightarrow 2}\left(\frac{5 x-8}{8-3 x}\right)^{\frac{3}{2 x-4}}=$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \lim _{x \rightarrow 2}\left(\frac{5 x-8}{8-3 x}\right) \frac{3}{2 x-4}=e^{\lim _{x \rightarrow 2}\left(\frac{5 x-8}{8-3 x}-1\right) \times \frac{3}{2 x-4}} \\ & =e^{\lim _{x \rightarrow 2} \frac{8(x-2) \times 3}{(8-3 x) 2(x-2)}}=e^6 \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
If \(\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{[(a-n) n x-\tan x] \sin n x}{x^2}=0,(n \neq 0)\)
then the minimum possible positive value of \(a\) is
questions.solution:
\(\begin{aligned}
& \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{((a-n) n x-\tan x) \sin n x}{x^2}=0, n \neq 0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \lim _{x \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{(a-n) n x}{x}-\frac{\tan x}{x}\right) \frac{\sin (n x)}{x}=0 \\
& \quad\left[\because \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin n x}{n x}=n\right] \\
& \Rightarrow \quad n\left(a n-n^2-1\right)=0 \Rightarrow \quad a=n+\frac{1}{n}, n \neq 0 \\
& \because \quad \frac{n+\frac{1}{n}}{2} \geq \sqrt{n \frac{1}{n}} \text { (by AM } \geq \text { GM) } \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \frac{a}{2} \geq 1 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad a \geq 2
\end{aligned}\)
\(\therefore\) Minimum possible positive value of \(a\) is 2. Hence, option (c) is correct.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{1^2}{n^3+1^3}+\frac{2^2}{n^3+2^3}+\ldots+\frac{1}{2 n}\right)=$
questions.solution:
$L=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}\left[\frac{1^2}{n^3+1^3}+\frac{2^2}{n^3+2^3}+\ldots+\frac{n^2}{n^3+n^3}\right]$ $=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{r=1}^n \frac{r^2}{n^3+r^3}=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{r=1}^n \frac{r^2}{n^3\left(1+\frac{r^3}{n^3}\right)}$ $=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{1}{n} \sum_{r=1}^n \frac{\left(\frac{r}{n}\right)^2}{1+\left(\frac{r}{n}\right)^3}=\int_0^1 \frac{x^2 d x}{1+x^3}$
$=\frac{1}{3} \log 2=\log \sqrt[3]{2}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: \(\lim _{x \rightarrow \pi} \frac{1-\sin x / 2}{\left(\cos \frac{x}{2}\right)\left(\cos \frac{x}{4}-\sin \frac{x}{4}\right)}=\)
questions.solution:
\(\lim _{x \rightarrow \pi} \frac{1-\sin \frac{x}{2}}{\left(\cos \frac{x}{2}\right)\left(\cos \frac{x}{4}-\sin \frac{x}{4}\right)}\)
Let \(\quad x=\pi+h, x \rightarrow \pi, h \rightarrow 0\)
\(\begin{gathered}
=\lim _{\substack{x \rightarrow \pi \\
h \rightarrow 0}} \frac{1-\sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}+\frac{h}{2}\right)}{\cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2}+\frac{h}{2}\right)\left(\cos \left(\frac{\pi+h}{4}\right)-\sin \left(\frac{\pi+h}{4}\right)\right)} \\
=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{1-\cos \frac{h}{2}}{-\sin \frac{h}{2} \cdot\left(\cos \frac{\pi}{4} \cos \frac{h}{4}-\sin \frac{\pi}{4} \sin \frac{h}{4}-\sin \frac{\pi}{4} \cdot \cos \frac{h}{4}-\cos \frac{\pi}{4} \cdot \sin \frac{h}{4}\right)}
\end{gathered}\)
\(\begin{aligned} & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sqrt{2}\left(1-\cos \frac{h}{2}\right)}{-\sin \frac{h}{2}\left\{\cos \frac{h}{4}-\sin \frac{h}{4}-\cos \frac{h}{4}-\sin \frac{h}{4}\right\}} \\ & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sqrt{2}\left(1-\cos \frac{h}{2}\right)}{\sin \frac{h}{2} \cdot\left(-2 \sin \frac{h}{4}\right)} \\ & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sqrt{2} \cdot 2 \sin ^2 \frac{h}{4}}{2 \cdot \sin \frac{h}{2} \times \sin \frac{h}{4}} \\ & =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \sqrt{2} \frac{\sin \frac{h}{4}}{\sin \frac{h}{2}}=\sqrt{2} \lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{\sin \frac{h}{4}}{4\left(\frac{h}{4}\right)} \cdot \frac{2(h / 2)}{\sin \frac{h}{2}} \\ & =\sqrt{2} \times \frac{2}{4} \cdot \lim _{h \rightarrow 0}\left(\frac{\sin \frac{h}{4}}{\frac{h}{4}}\right)\left(\frac{\frac{h}{2}}{\sin \frac{h}{2}}\right) \\ & =\sqrt{2} \times \frac{2}{4} \times 1 \times 1=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\end{aligned}\)
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi>tan</mi><mo>⁡</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>3</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math>, then <math> <mrow> <msup> <mi>f</mi> <mo>′</mo> </msup> <mfenced> <mrow> <mfrac> <mi>π</mi> <mn>4</mn>  </mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow></math> (where, <math><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mo>.</mo></mrow></mfenced></math> is the greatest integer function)
questions.solution:
LHD: f'π4=limh→0+fπ4−h−fπ4−h
=limh→0+⁡tan⁡π4-h2⁡-tan⁡π42-h
=limh→0+⁡0-1-h→∞
∴ f'π4 does not exist.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>g</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>h</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>12</mn></mrow></mfrac></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi>lim</mi><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></munder><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mrow><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>g</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mi>h</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math> is
questions.solution:
Given: fx=2x-3, gx=x-3x+4 and hx=-22x+1x2+x-12=-22x+1x-3x+4.

We have fx+gx+hx=x2-4x+17-4x-2x2+x-12

=x2-8x+15x2+x-12=x-3x-5x-3x+4=x-5x+4

Now limx→3fx+gx+hx

=limx→3x-5x+4=-27
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mtext>lim</mtext><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></munder><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>sin</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mo>cos</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>ln</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>·</mo><msup><mo>tan</mo><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></math> is
questions.solution:
limx→01+sinx-cosx+ℓn1-xx·tan2x

=limx→01+sinx-cosx+ℓn1-xx300    [ ∵  lim x→0 x tan x =1 ]

=limx→0cosx+sinx-11-x3x2         00     [using L'hospital rule]

=limx→0-sinx+cosx-1(1-x)26x         00    [Using L'hospital rule again]

=limx→0-cosx-sinx-2(1-x)36     [Using L'hospital rule again]

= - 1 - 2 6 = - 1 2
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mspace width="0.5em"></mspace><munder><mtext>lim</mtext><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></munder><mfrac><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mrow><mn>6</mn><mn>0</mn><mn>0</mn><mn>0</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mfenced><mrow><mo>sin </mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mn>6</mn><mn>0</mn><mn>0</mn><mn>0</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mfenced><mrow><mo>sin</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mn>6</mn><mn>0</mn><mn>0</mn><mn>0</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac></math> is
questions.solution:
limx→0⁡x6000-sinx6000x2sinx6000x6000.x600000

limx→0x6000-sinx6000x6002

= -limx→0sinx6000-x6000x6002

=-limx→0sinxx6000-1x2

=-limx→06000sinxx5999xcosx-sinxx22x (By L-Hospital Rule)

=-60002limx→0xcosx-sinxx3

 
=-3000 limx→0cosx-x sinx-cosx3x2 

=1000

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
Let $a>0$ be a real number. Then the limit $\lim _{x \rightarrow 2} \frac{a^{x}+a^{3-x}-\left(a^{2}+a\right)}{a^{3-x}-a^{x / 2}}$
is
questions.solution:
$\lim _{x \rightarrow 2} \frac{a^{x}+a^{3-x}-\left(a^{2}+a\right)}{a^{3-x}-a^{x / 2}}$
$\lim _{x \rightarrow 2} \frac{\left(a^{x}-a\right)\left(a^{x / 2}-a\right)\left(a^{x / 2}+a\right)}{\left(a-a^{x / 2}\right)\left(a^{2}+a^{x}+a^{x / 2} \cdot a\right)}$
$\lim _{x \rightarrow 2}-\frac{\left(a^{x}-a\right)\left(a^{x / 2}+a\right)}{a^{2}+a^{x}+a^{x / 2} \cdot a}$
$=\frac{2}{3}(1-a)$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of <math><munder><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></munder><mfrac><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">tan</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">cos</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
costan⁡x-cos⁡x=2sinx+tan⁡x2sinx-tan⁡x2
⇒limx→0costan⁡x-cos⁡x4x4=limx→02sinx+tan⁡x2sinx-tan⁡tan⁡x24x4
=limx→02sinx+tan⁡x2sinx-tan⁡x24x4x+tan⁡x2x-tan⁡x2x2-tan2x4
=18limx→0x2-tan2xx4
=18limx→0x2-x+x33+215x5+....2x4
=18limx→01x21-1+x23+215x4+....2=-112
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of <math><mrow><mrow><munder><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">lim</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><msup><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup></mrow></munder></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mfrac><mrow><msqrt><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></msqrt><mo>-</mo><msqrt><mn>4</mn><mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow></msqrt></mrow><mrow><msqrt><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></msqrt></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mrow></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
We have, limx→1-⁡π-2sin-1⁡x1-x2
=limx→1-⁡π-2sin-1⁡x1-x×π+2sin-1⁡xπ+2sin-1⁡x×2
=limx→1-⁡22π2-sin-1⁡x1-xπ+2sin-1⁡x
=limx→1-⁡22cos-1⁡x1-x⋅12π
=limθ→0+⁡22θ2sin⁡θ2⋅12π [Putting x=cos⁡θ ]
=4222π=2π
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of <math><munder><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">∞</mi></mrow></munder><msup><mrow><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><msup><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn><msup><mrow><mn>0</mn></mrow><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></mrow></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>10</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>10</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup></math> is
questions.solution:
limx→∞11x+21x+31x+...+101x1010x
=limy→0+1y+2y+3y+...+10y1010y where, y=1x
=elimy→0+10y1y+2y+3y+...+10y10-1
=elimy→0+1y+2y+3y+...+10y-10y
=elimy→0+1y-1y+2y-1y+3y-1y+...+10y-1y
=elog1+log2+log3+...+log10
=elog1⋅2⋅3.....10=10!
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>→</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></munder><mfrac><mrow><mi>x</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mfenced close="}" open="{" separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfrac></math> is equal to (where <math><mfenced close="}" open="{" separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> denotes the fractional part of <math><mi>x</mi></math>)
questions.solution:
LHL=limx→1-xtanxx-1
Let, x=1-h, as x→1-, h→0+
⇒LHL=limh→0+1-htan1-h-h
∴LHL=limh→0+1-h-htan1=-∞
Now, RHL=limx→1+xtanxx-1
Let, x=1+h, as x→1+, h→0+
⇒RHL=limh→0+1+htan1+hh
⇒RHL=limh→0+1+htanhh=limh→0+1+h
∴RHL=1+0=1
Since, LHL≠RHL
∴ the limit of the function does not exist at x=1
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
